ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Ezme. Mapped to the nearest parent class 'vegetable salad food product' (FOODON_03311737) as a defensible approximate anchor. Ezme is a finely chopped Turkish tomato, pepper, and herb salad served as mezze.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tomato-based salad / vegetable salad — mixed chopped vegetables with dressing
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not contain a specific code for Ezme. Code A00LC (mixed vegetable salad) is used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. Ezme is a raw chopped tomato, pepper, onion, and herb preparation with olive oil.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Ezme or a sufficiently close Turkish vegetable salad preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard side dish serving of Ezme is approximately 150 g, equivalent to a small bowl served alongside grilled meats, kebabs, or flatbread as part of a Turkish mezze spread.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Tomato-based vegetable salad with olive oil dressing (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite estimate)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Ezme exists in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore Foodo, or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for raw tomatoes (FDC ID 170457), raw green bell pepper (FDC ID 170108), raw red bell pepper (FDC ID 170107), raw onion (FDC ID 170000), fresh parsley (FDC ID 170416), and olive oil (FDC ID 171413), combined in proportions consistent with standard Ezme recipes (approximately 40% tomato, 20% mixed peppers, 15% onion, 10% parsley, 10% olive oil, 5% lemon juice and seasonings). The input record values (energy 80 kcal, protein 1.67 g, fat 5.33 g, carbohydrate 6.67 g, sugar 4.0 g, sodium 166.67 mg, dietary fibre 2.0 g, calcium 20 mg, iron 0.67 mg, cholesterol 0.0 mg, saturated fat 0.67 g, trans fat 0.0 g, vitamin C 26.67 mg per 100 g) are consistent with this USDA composite estimate and are retained with medium confidence. Sodium reflects added salt typical of restaurant preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on mixed raw vegetable salad with olive oil dressing
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ezme in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. GI of 38 is estimated by analogy with mixed raw vegetable salads with olive oil dressing as reported in Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Raw tomatoes have a GI of approximately 15–30; peppers and onions are similarly low-GI; olive oil suppresses postprandial glucose response. A composite GI of 38 (low category) is a conservative and defensible estimate. GL per 150 g serving = (38 × 6.67 g carbohydrate × 1.5) / 100 = 3.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 166.67 mg/100 g; high potassium from tomatoes, peppers, and onions (~280 mg/100 g); olive oil provides unsaturated fats
hypertension.guidance: Ezme is suitable for hypertension management: sodium at 166.67 mg/100 g is below the 200 mg threshold, and the high potassium content from tomatoes, peppers, and onions supports DASH diet principles; however, patients should request reduced added salt and verify that red pepper paste or pomegranate molasses used in restaurant preparations do not significantly elevate sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~38), low carbohydrate 6.67 g/100 g, dietary fibre 2.0 g/100 g, no refined carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ezme is well-suited for Type 2 Diabetes management: its estimated low GI of 38, minimal carbohydrate load (6.67 g/100 g), and dietary fibre (2.0 g/100 g) from raw vegetables produce a low glycaemic load of approximately 3.8 per 150 g serving; pomegranate molasses contributes natural sugars and should be used in moderation if strict glycaemic control is required.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Olive oil provides monounsaturated fats (~3.8 g/100 g); saturated fat low at 0.67 g/100 g; zero trans fat; vitamin C 26.67 mg/100 g as antioxidant
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ezme is cardioprotective: olive oil contributes monounsaturated fatty acids (~3.8 g/100 g) and oleocanthal polyphenols that reduce LDL oxidation; saturated fat is negligible at 0.67 g/100 g; trans fat is absent; and vitamin C (26.67 mg/100 g) from peppers and tomatoes provides antioxidant support for endothelial health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory olive oil polyphenols; vitamin C 26.67 mg/100 g; nightshade vegetables (tomatoes, peppers) may trigger sensitivity in some RA patients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ezme may benefit most RA patients through olive oil's oleocanthal and vitamin C antioxidants, but patients with known solanine sensitivity should monitor symptoms when consuming tomatoes, green peppers, red peppers, and red pepper flakes, and may consider requesting a nightshade-reduced version.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ezme (also known as Acılı Ezme) is a traditional Turkish spicy tomato and pepper salad served as a mezze or condiment alongside grilled meats, kebabs, shawarma, and flatbread. It is prepared by finely chopping or pulsing fresh tomatoes, green peppers, red peppers, onion, and parsley, then dressing with olive oil, lemon juice, sumac, red pepper flakes, and optionally pomegranate molasses. The result is a vibrant, tangy, and mildly spicy relish with a chunky texture and bright red colour. Nutritionally, Ezme is low in calories (80 kcal/100 g), low in carbohydrates (6.67 g/100 g), and rich in monounsaturated fats from olive oil (approximately 3.8 g/100 g). It provides meaningful vitamin C (26.67 mg/100 g) from peppers and tomatoes, dietary fibre (2.0 g/100 g), and potassium (~280 mg/100 g). Its estimated glycaemic index of 38 places it firmly in the low-GI category, making it suitable for Type 2 Diabetes and weight management. Sodium at 166.67 mg/100 g is moderate and below the 200 mg threshold relevant to hypertension guidelines. Ezme is naturally vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, and halal. It is a staple of Turkish cuisine and is widely served in Turkish restaurants globally, including in Singapore's Turkish and Middle Eastern dining establishments.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Ezme exists in any approved primary nutrient database (HPB Singapore, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from a USDA SR Legacy composite of raw ingredient components in proportions consistent with standard Ezme recipes. The input record values are consistent with this composite estimate and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed international GI tables. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite of raw tomatoes (FDC 170457), green bell pepper (FDC 170108), red bell pepper (FDC 170107), onion (FDC 170000), parsley (FDC 170416), and olive oil (FDC 171413) in standard Ezme proportions. No exact approved-source entry for Ezme exists. 2. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient breakdown for this composite Turkish vegetable salad preparation is not individually reported as a combined dish entry in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16; individual raw ingredient entries (FDC 170457, 170108, 170107, 170000, 170416, 171413) do not provide a reliable composite figure for these micronutrients without a validated recipe-level calculation tool, and no such composite is available in an approved source. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg and vitamin_d_mcg are set to 0.0: Ezme is a plant-based preparation containing no animal products; vitamin B12 and vitamin D are naturally zero in this food. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: Ezme contains no animal-derived ingredients; cholesterol is naturally zero. trans_fat_g is 0.0: no hydrogenated fats are used; confirmed negligible per USDA olive oil and raw vegetable entries. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g (3.8 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.6 g) are estimated from the olive oil component proportion per USDA FDC 171413 fatty acid profile. 6. potassium_mg (~280 mg/100 g) and vitamin_a_mcg_rae (~42 mcg/100 g) are estimated from the USDA composite ingredient proportions. 7. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate based on mixed raw vegetable salad with olive oil dressing per Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for Ezme was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. Sodium of 166.67 mg/100 g reflects added salt typical of restaurant preparation; home-prepared versions may be lower. 9. The input record listed 'country_of_origin: Singapore' which reflects the serving context (Singapore restaurant); the true culinary origin is Turkey and this is reflected in cuisine and region_of_origin fields. 10. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean serving context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per Project Veritas multilingual rules for dishes served in Singapore. 11. Aliases self-check: all string values in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array — confirmed consistent.
